Vandy ED MD Stark ntfd, determination of efforts called at approx 01:48.

Medic 9 dispatched emergent to a headache. Arrived on scene & met by staff at the front door that stated ''Pt. coded on the second floor. 
Found a 68 y.o. white male supine on the bathroom floor unresponsive & being ventilated via BVM by Stallworth staff. Pt. doesn't have a radial pulse but has a femoral pulse present. 
Pt. has a patent airway, Agonal respiration, good chest rise & fall with assisted ventilation. MD stated ''Pt. was awake & alert approx 10 mins ago when he suddenly started c/o a severe headache, became lethargic, & unresponsive''. Pt. didn't fall, he was assisted down to the floor per staff. Vital signs PTA BP 150/70 , HR 46, & 12 lead sinus tach. Pt. has a recent hx. of CVA, AVM rupture with subarachnoid hemorrhage. Pt. placed on long backboard & secured with straps. Moved Pt. on backboard from the floor to the stretcher. Secured with straps & rails. Full head to toe assessment conducted. EYES: equal pinpoint. Pt. initially placed on cardiac monitor Defib pads Sinus Brady HR 46. OPA removed to place advanced airway i-gel & while inserting the i-gel the Pt. still had gag reflex intact, i-get removed, & continued ventilation via BVM. 18ga IV L.AC & 16ga R.AC established. Pt. received Narcan 2mg I.N. with no improvement. 12 lead obtained Narrow complex sinus bradycardia. Narcan 0.5mg IV & Atropine 0.5mg IV administered with no improvement. Pt. transported emergent to Vanderbilt ER with firefighter on board. Arrived to destination without incident. Moved Pt. on backboard from the stretcher to the bed ER Trauma bay 2. Pt. documentation & care transferred to nursing staff.

PRESENTED WITH A 58 Y/O W/M ACTING EXTREMELY ERRATIC. HE IS AA&OX4 AND DIAPHORETIC. HE IS MAKING JERKING MOVEMENTS AND ADVISES THAT HE DOES NOT FEEL RIGHT. HE WAS SECURED TO COT WITHOUT COMPROMISE TO ABCS OR PMS. V/S LISTED, CEBBS, PUPILS PINPOINT, ST ON EKG, NO ACUTE ST CHANGES ON 12 LEAD, BG 101 MG/DL. AN IV WAS ESTABLISHED AND HE WAS ADMINISTERED 1 MG OF NARCAN. THE JERKING MOVEMENTS STOPPED AND HE WAS SPEAKING CLEARLY. HE ADMITTED TO TAKING A PERCOCET THAT HE OBTAINED OFF THE STREET. HE WAS TRANSPORTED NON-EMERGENCY TO CENTENNIAL ER WITH RADIO REPORT GIVEN. HE WAS RELEASED TO STAFF WITHOUT INCIDENT.

Chief complaint: unresponsive, possibly secondary to ETOH overdose. 

History: we were approached by a bartender and a bouncer stating they found an unresponsive man behind their bar. 

Assessment: upon initial approach, patient was found sitting upright with his head dropped between his knees. Lifted patient's head and he did not respond. Patient was unresponsive: GCS 10 with non-labored breathing and skin was pink, warm and diaphoretic. Patient had copious amounts of chewing tobacco in his mouth. While securing the patient to the stretcher, he became combative. Physically restrained the patient to the stretcher with soft restraints (roll gauze). This helped manage the patient enough to put him and in the ambulance. The patient continued to be combative and it became clear that the soft restraints were not sufficient to control the patient. Administered 5mg of Versed IM. Minutes later the patient ceased his combative behavior and was GCS 3. Patient began dry heaving. Administered 4mg of Versed IV. Lung sounds were clear and equal bilaterally. 3 lead EKG, which was assessed due to the patient's unresponsive state. Patient remained GCS 3 (chemically sedated) throughout duration of the call.

Transport: transported the patient non-emergency to Centennial Medical Center. Upon arrival: gave report to, obtained signature from and transferred care of patient to ED RN.

Medic 39 dispatched and responded emergent to pt location. MNPD was on scene and made initial pt contact. EMS was delayed on scene due to potential hazards. The pt was able to walk to the ambulance without incident and was properly secured to the stretcher with straps and rails, good PMS in all extremities. He stated that he was paranoid he was going to be in trouble and kept repeating the question: ''Am I going to jail?'' The pt was continuously monitored enroute and remained hemodynamically stable. ARRIVE ON SCENE WITH ENGINE 36 FOR A REPORTED UNRESPONSIVE 74 YEAR OLD FEMALE. UPON EMS ARRIVAL, THE PT'S FAMILY HAD MADE ENTRY INTO THE HOUSE. THE FAMILY ADVISES THE ONLY SIGNIFICANT MEDICAL HISTORY THEY ARE AWARE OF IS SEVERE PSYCHIATRIC DISEASE FOR WHICH SHE WAS RECENTLY HOSPITALIZED FOR. THE FAMILY STATES THE LAST TIME THEY SAW HER WAS 2 DAYS AGO AND THEY WERE WORRIED SHE MIGHT HAVE ATTEMPTED SUICIDE. FIRST RESPONDERS ON SCENE ADVISE THAT THE PT WAS INITIALLY LYING SUPINE ON HER BED SURROUNDED BY VOMIT, UNRESPONSIVE AND NOT BREATHING ADEQUATELY. INITIALLY THE PT'S O2 WAS FOUND TO BE 70% ON ROOM AIR. THE PT WAS MOVED TO THE COT AND SECURED IN SEMI FOWLERS WITH STRAPS AND SIDE RAILS AND MOVED TO THE AMBULANCE. HEAD TO TOE ASSESSMENT PERFORMED, VITAL SIGNS OBTAINED, 2MG OF NARCAN ADMINISTERED INTRANASALLY, AND 3 LEAD MONITOR PLACED ON THE PT. 4L OF OXYGEN ADMINISTERED AS WELL VIA NASAL CANNULA WHICH IMPROVED HER SATURATION. UNABLE TO ASSESS PMS DUE TO PT'S CONDITION, THOUGH SHE WAS MOVING ALL EXTREMITIES SPONTANEOUSLY AND EQUALLY THROUGHOUT TRANSPORT. RHONCHI NOTED BILATERALLY PARTICULARLY IN THE PT'S UPPER FIELDS. ABDOMEN IS SOFT AND NON TENDER. LOWER EXTREMITIES ARE NORMAL. PT TRANSPORTED NON EMERGENCY TO SUMMIT ER WHERE SHE WAS PLACED IN A ROOM AND REPORT WAS GIVEN TO THE NURSING STAFF ASSUMING CARE AT THE BEDSIDE.

Medic 38 dispatched to 309 Bonnacroft Dr for a Cardiac Arrest.

59 y/o male C/O of Cardiac Arrest, witnessed by patient's Son, Son stats patient didn't not C/O any Chest Pain or shortness of breath prior to becoming unresponsive.

Patient has a medical history of Diabetes, CABG. Unable to obtain any medications or allergies.

Upon medic 38 arrival, patient was found lying supine on the long spine board, NFD Engine 32 performing CPR and Ventilating patient, Patient is unresponsive, not breathing and has no pulse. Showing Ventricular Fibrillation on the cardiac monitor in lead 2, Blood Glucose 320 mg/dl, Pupils 5 mm sluggish to light response. 

ABC's, vitals, CPR 15:2 compression ratio, ventilate patient via BVM, Oxygen 15 lpm via BVM, Defibrillate 4 times at 200 joules after 2 miutes of CPR and when patient was in Vetricular Fibrillation, Patient would periods of showing Asystole on the Cardiac monitor, then convert back into V-Fib after 2 minutes of CPR, 3 mg of Epinephrine 1:10,000 IV push, 100 mg Lidocaine IV Push,  2 mg of Narcan IV push. Patient was intubated with a 8.0 mm ET Tube, secured with tube holder at 24 cm at the teeth, present sounds present and rise / fall of chest with ventilations, No Epigastric sounds. ETC02 24 when patient was intubated.

Medic 38 transported emergency to Summit Medical Center ER with no further changes in patient condition, Patient remained in cardiac arrest, Patient care transferred over to ER, resport given, no further patient contact.

Medic 6 responded to the scene for the cardiac arrest.  Upon arrival crew found 27 year old male lying in supine position in bed in the bedroom with patients family and PD on-scene.  Patient is unresponsive with agonal breathing, but a strong radial pulse present.  Patients family patient overdosed this evening.  Patients family further stated that they last saw patient about 6 to 7 hours ago.  Patients family stated that they found patient in bed not responding and barely breathing.  PD stated that they administered patient 4 mg of Narcan via IN.  Upon assessment crew noted patients to be cold and cyanotic.  Crew further noted patients pupils to be pinpoint bilaterally.  Crew began ventilating patient via BVM with supplemental oxygen.  Crew obtained patients vital signs and placed patient on cardiac monitor.  Crew suctioned patients airway and administered patient oxygen via NRB at 10 lpm.  Crew further noted patients skin improved and pupils became dilated.  Crew noted patient became more awake.  Patient A&O to person only with GCS of 12.  Crew established IV access.  Southern Hills Hospital contacted with no further questions.  While en-route to the hospital patient took off the NRB mask and was maintaining oxygen saturation on room air.  Crew reassessed patient and repeated vital signs with no other obvious significant changes in patients condition.  Transfer of care to ER RN with patient in bed, ER #11.  JEB

ARRIVE ON SCENE FOR A FEMALE THAT WAS REPORTEDLY IN CARDIAC ARREST. UPON EMS ARRIVAL, THE PT WAS FOUND LYING SUPINE ON THE FLOOR OF HER APARTMENT UNRESPONIVE WITH AGONAL RESPIRATIONS AND A STRONG PULSE. THE PT'S FAMILY HAD STARTED CPR, BUT EMS DID NOT CONTINUE DUE TO THE PRESENCE OF A PULSE. THE PT'S AIRWAY WAS OPENED AND VENTILATIONS WERE ASSISTED WITH A BVM. 2MG OF NARCAN ADMINISTERED IM AND THE PT WAS MOVED TO THE COT VIA MANSAC WHERE SHE WAS SECURED IN SEMI FOWLERS WITH STRAPS AND SIDE RAILS AND MOVED TO THE AMBULANCE. HEAD TO TOE ASSESSMENT PERFORMED AND VITAL SIGNS OBTAINED. 3 LEAD CARDIAC MONITOR PLACED ON THE PT AND A 26F NPA WAS PLACED IN HER RIGHT NOSTRIL. PT'S OVERALL ASSESSMENT REVEAL RHONCHI AND CRACKLING IN HER LUNGS BILATERALLY. PT'S ASSESSMENT WAS OTHERWISE NORMAL. PT'S BREATHING STATUS GREATLY IMPROVED, THOUGH SHE REMAINED UNCONSCIOUS FOR MOST OF THE TRANSPORT. PT TRANSPORTED EMERGENCY TRAFFIC TO ST THOMAS MIDTOWN. UPON ARRIVAL AT THE HOSPITAL, THE PT BECAME RESPONSIVE TO VERBAL STIMULI. PT MOVED TO A ROOM AND REPORT WAS GIVEN TO THE NURSING STAFF ASSUMING CARE AT THE BEDSIDE.

[GENERAL IMPRESSION]
Upon arrival, found patient sitting on sidewalk. He was awake and alert.

[COMPLAINT / HISTORY]
He snorted a powdered substance that he believed to be cocaine -- he began feeling dizzy, tired, and not right afterward. He believed that it may have actually been heroin or fentanyl. He received narcan internasal from the mission while he was still awake and alert. He felt nauseated afterward.

[ASSESSMENT]
AAOx4.CEBBS.
Vitals as noted.

[TX/RX]
Patient placed on cot, secured, and loaded.

[REASSESSMENT]
Patient remained unchanged.

Medic 6 was dispatched to the location for a 29 y/o M for a heat exposure. Upon arrival, the pt was sitting in his car. The pt had open alcohol in the car. The pt's wife stated the pt had been drinking all night and slept in his car. The car had the windows down but was not running. The pt was asleep, but was arousable. The pt would wake up to verbal response, but wouldn't speak and then he would fall back asleep. The pt was assisted to the stretcher and taken to the ambulance. The pt's VS were obtained, as well as a 4-lead. The pt had pinpoint pupils so he was given Narcan. The pt was monitored throughout transport. The pt woke up from the second dose of Narcan and was then AAOX4. The pt stated he took a drug but wouldn't tell me what drug. He said he refuses to tell me and stated ''I don't have to''. The pt was still barely unable to stay awake, but he could answer questions. The pt was taken to a room upon ER arrival. Report was given to the RN and MD, the RN signed, and the care of the pt was transferred over.

Medic 05 responded immediately to the jail for possible overdose. Arrive on scene to find the pt in the medical bay at metro jail. Pt report was given and care transferred to ER staff. Signatures obtained and medic 05 returned to service.

Prior to transport the Pt. woke is alert to self & maintaining his how airway. Pt. admitted to shooting up Fentanyl in his right hip/pelvic area. U/A the pt was found responsive to painful stimuli. Home health stated that she has been sleeping since her shift started at 0700. Upon arrival we find Pt lying supine on the floor. Pt's dad is doing CPR. However Pt is breathing and has a pulse. PT is unresponsive, cyanotic with snoring respirations. Pt has pin point pupils. Pt was administered 2 MG of Narcan in the right thigh. Pt was placed on a pulse ox and his room air was 88. Pt however responded quickly to the Narcan. Pt's breathing improved and his O2 sat climbed into the 90's quickly. PT woke up shortly after. Pt admitted to Heroine use. Pt states that he was getting clean and had been sober about a month. Pt had an impulse to use that he couldn't fight. Pt was helped to the cot, positioned and secured with straps. Pt was loaded into the unit. Pt was placed on the monitor with vitals obtained. Pt was transported to Summit. During transit Pt had no changes. Upon arrival to the ER staff assumed Pt care.

Emergency response to residence for complaint of an overdose.   Upon arrival, pt (23 yoa F) found on the floor conscious and breathing. Medic 37 responded to address above for a male that was unresponsive. When on scene we found TR37 with the pt. The crew from truck 37 stated that the pt was unresponsive and was only breathing about 6 times a minutes. The Crew stated that they then gave the pt 4mg Narcan. When at the pt side we found the pt still unresponsive and was still breathing about 6 times a minutes. We then put the pt in a man sac and moved him down stairs and over to the cot. After getting the pt on the cot we then started to secure the pt and he started to become responsive and started to fight us. The pt was very aggressive and was trying to punch and kick at us. We tried calming the pt down but he would not relax. We then moved the pt to the unit where we had to use soft restraints on the pt. The pt still tried to bite use after we restrained him. After talking the pt down he then started to relax and was asking what happened. We then advised the pt that a family member found him at home laying in his bed and was not breathing. We then told the pt that we had to use a drug call Narcan to reverse the effects of the Drug he used. The pt had some white powder around nostrils. I then asked the pt what drug did he use and he stated that he though that he was snorting Heroin. We then finished assessing the pt and then we started to transport emergent to St. Thomas West. During the transport the pt continued to calm down but continue to fall asleep and wake up and be very agitated. When there we took the pt to room 6 Where we then moved the pt over to the bed and then I gave my report to the staff and then I handed over pt care to the nursing staff.

Medic 23 was dispatched and responded emergency traffic to 5800 Maudina Ave for a 42 year old male with chest pain. On scene ems crew took the cot and monitor to the entrance of the apartment complex. We went upstairs to apartment D4 where the pt resides. Ems was met at the door by the pt. 42 year old male awake alert and oriented. Pt did not appear to be in any distress and had no issues breathing on room air. Pt stated that he has been having chest pains since this morning around 2-3 AM. He stated that he has no unknown cardiac issues. He stated he feels like his heart rate is up some and that its causing just a sharp pain in the left side of his chest. Pt was assisted down the steps to the cot. Pt was secured and taken to unit. Pt was placed on 4 lead, 12 lead, bp cuff, and pulse ox. Pts HR was elevated on ECG but no abnormalities were found. He told ems that he had been using cocaine prior to the pain starting and after. He denied any history of SVT so it was nor presumed due to the drug use. Pt transport was started to St Thomas West. En route pts vitals were monitored and remained stable within normal limits. Pts blood pressure was staying around or below 100 systolic and the pt advised that was around normal for him. He was given 324 mg of ASA by mouth per protocol and nitro was held to to borderline low blood pressure. Pt provided ems with his demographics and history. At West pt was taken into facility on cot to registration. Pt was then taken to room 18. Pt was assisted to bed. Report was given to staff and signatures were obtained. EOR

Medic 10 responded emergency/ immediately to the dispatched location with a dispatched complaint of overdose, arrived on scene without incident. Adult first in bag, portable o2, stretcher and cardiac monitor were brought to the entrance of the dwelling. Upon entering ems was met by the homeowners who are letting the patient stay at there house. Per the home owners the patient has been isolating himself since Saturday and has consumed 12-15 bottles of vodka, using unknown pills and inhaling co2 cylinders. Upon entering the patients room ems was presented with a male patient supine on the bed with multiple empty co2 cylinders on the blanket with syringes being noted on the nightstand. The patient is very excited and uncooperative. The patient is oriented to person and place but is not oriented to time or event. The patient is refusing treatment and transport, requested for law enforcement and district 15 to respond. After deescalating the situation the patient has agreed to be transported and is cooperating with ems. Assisted the the patient down the stairs using a underarm technique x 2 personnel. Placed the patient on the ems stretcher in a semi-fowlers position, rails up x2, straps x5 with the stretcher being secured to the medic unit using a commercial device. Assessment performed and baseline vitals were obtained with findings being noted in the full pcr. ECG/ 12 lead obtained due to the patients elevated heart rate. Transported non emergency with the patient being continually monitored during transport. The patient denies any past medical hx, daily meds or allergies. Radio report called with a 5 mine eta, arrived at or destination without incident. The patient self transferred to bed 5, semi-fowlers position, rails up x2 and bedside report was given. No changes in the patients state during transport. Patient care transferred and signed for as noted in the full pcr.

FF found that pt had pulse and discontinued cpr. FF assisted ventilations using bvm. FF administered  a total of 4mg narcan intranasally. Pt woke and became alert. Assisted pt to cot, secured. Moved pt to unit, obtained vs and iv as charted. Became irritable with crew. Resp distress not noted. SKin was p/w/d. Pupils perrl. Lungs cebbs. Transported non to CMC. During transport, pt became verbally responsive but was maintaining airway. AT dest., moved pt toer bed 7. Transferred care to rn.

MEDIC 31 RESPONDED TO 24 Y/O/WM PT FOUND UNRESPONSIVE AND AGONAL POST OPIATE OVERDOSE. PRIOR TO ARRIVAL, PT WAS ADMIN NARCAN. UPON ARRIVAL HE WAS LAYING SUPINE IN THE STREET, ALERT TO VERBAL, UNABLE TO ANSWER QUESTIONS APPROPRIATE. NO VISIBLE SIGNS OF TRAUMA OR BLEEDING. INITIAL EXAM NOTED. PT PLACED ON COT C/ STRAPS AND LOADED INTO AMBULANCE. VS NOTED. PT PLACED ON MONITOR. PT ADMITTED TO OPIATE ABUSE. HE WAS TX TO THE HOSPITAL SFPOC AND MONITORED ENROUTE. CONDITION IMPROVED UPON ARRIVAL.

SQ26-B was en route to a medical alarm and came upon a driver slumped over steering wheel in his truck. SQ26 staged behind vehicle to protect from oncoming traffic. SQ26 attempted to raise driver buy shining light in driver's face and knocking heavily on the window several times. Driver remained unconscious.

SQ26 notified dispatch to send an additional unit to the medical alarm with MED33 (FFD241117155749) and requested to be placed on this call at Bell Road and Cane Ridge road.

All truck doors were locked and crew was unable to visualize whether occupant was breathing or not. SQ26 broke back left door window to gain access in to the vehicle and unlocked the truck from the inside. SQ26 opened driver door and placed vehicle in park and turned vehicle off. SQ26 was able to wake up the driver with a sternal rub. At this time, PD arrived and took over the scene. Driver was awake and speaking with PD with slurred speech, alcohol was present in vehicle. Driver was breathing normally with patent airway and said he had just fallen asleep at the wheel. PD took driver into custody. SQ26 left the scene after PD staged behind the truck in the middle of the road. SQ26 back in service.

Arrived with Medic 10 to find a female complaining of drug use, intended to use heroin but shot up an unknown substance.  Complaining of tingling in arms and legs.  A, A, O x 3.  Skin warm, dry, pink.  PERRL.  No JVD.  Chest rise equal, nonlabored.  Felt ''SOB''.  Abd soft, flat, nontender.  PMS x 4.  Assessed.   Assisted in loading.

Dispatched to the above location emergency traffic from base for a unresponsive pt. Upon arrival the pt was found in his vehicle and responsive to painful stimuli. Upon initial assessment the pt was noted as having pin point pupils, diaphoretic and kept becoming unresponsive. Pt was given 1mg of Narcan IN per protocol as noted below. The pt then became alert and was assisted out of the vehicle. The pt was placed onto the cot and loaded into Medic 35. We began assessing the pt. Pt was placed onto the monitor and a complete set of vitals were obtained as noted below. Iv was started using an aseptic technique as noted below. Pt again began to go in and out of consciousness. Upon assessment pt was still noted as having pin point pupils. Pt was given 1 mg of narcan Iv using an aseptic technique as noted below. Pt regained consciousness and became AAO x 4. Pt states he smoked heroine a short time ago. Transport began non emergency to SHH. During transport the pt remained AAO x 4. A complete set of vitals were reassessed as noted below. Report was called into the receiving facility without incident. Upon arrival to the receiving facility the pt was transferred from the cot to the bed. Report was given at bedside without incident. Medic 35 obtained all appropriate signatures and returned to service.

medic 33 on scene to a 39y/o female lying outside of an apartment complex with boyfriend, Fast and Engine 32 on scene. Upon assessment, Fast 32 initiated CPR due to no pulse with agonal respirations. AED was applied and informed to start CPR. Boyfriend inform pt is a known drug user. Pt was given 2mg of Narcan IM and Ventilated via BVM. No shock advised Pt was carried to cot via mansac and secured to cot with straps and rails up. Prior to entering ambulance. Pts respirations improved with a pulse noted. Further eval in ambulance.

Pt was placed on high flow O2 via NRB.12 lead obtained. glucose obtained. Pt continued to become more oriented to verbal stimuli throughout tranport. Pt was taken to Summit ER w/o incident and monitored throughout transport. Pt care transferred over to care staff on duty with report given to signed nurse.

M28 DISPATCHED IMMEDIATE TO A LOCAL MOTEL SECONDARY TO REPORTS OF AN 18 YO F SUBJECT WHO OVERDOSED. AOS TO FIND THE SUBJECT WALKING OUT OF THE ROOM UNDER HER OWN POWER. SHE ADMITTED TO XANAX, METH, AND HEROIN USE. SHE WAS ABLE TO ANSWER ALL QUESTIONING APPROPRIATELY, BUT WOULD NOD OFF IF NOT INTERACTED WITH. AUNT STATED THAT SHE FOUND THE SUBJECT OVERDOSED AND NAKED IN THE BED. NO PERTINENT HX. SHE AGREED TO ASSESSMENT, BUT ADAMANTLY REFUSED TRANSPORT, EVEN WITH D15 ON SCENE. VITALS AS NOTED. PERRL, HEENT CLEAR, NO JVD/TD, NO PAIN, CEBBS, AB SOFT, SKIN NORM CAP REFILL NORM. BGL 143. STROKE NEG. NO SEIZURE WITNESSED. RISKS EXPLAINED, UP TO AND INCLUDING OVERDOSE, POSSIBLE NEUROLOGICAL ISSUES, AND DEATH, BUT SHE CONTINUED TO REFUSE. SHE PLANNED TO REMAIN ON SCENE WITH HER AUNT. SHE DID NOT PLAN TO SEEK MEDICAL TREATMENT. SHE AGREED TO REASSESS 911 IF NEEDED. REFUSAL SIGNED BY SUBJECT AND WITNESSED BY AUNT. RETURNED TO SERVICE.

Dispatched to above for OD. UOA Pt was unconscious in a chair soaked from his friends pouring water on him. Pt minimal snoring respirations, pupils pinpoint. Pt placed on stretcher, secured x5, IN narcan given. Monitor shows sinus tach, BP elevated, glucose normal. Pt woke up and was able to give us his info but otherwise just wanted to sleep. O2 via NC was applied with mask. Pt was easy to wake and breathing normally. Pt remained stable throughout transport, report given to ED via radio, taken to ED bed 17. RN and Paramedic signed.

PATIENT LOADED ON THE STRETCHER AND SECURED X3 STRAPS X2 RAILS IN A SEMI-FOLWERS POSITION AND MOVED TO THE AMBULANCE.  NO SIGNS OF OBVIOUS TRAUMA NOTED TO THE BODY. VITALS OBTAINED,  MONITOR APPLIED. PATIENT HAD NO BELONGINGS EXCEPT FOR ID THAT WAS FOUND IN CLOTHING. PATIENT CARE RELEASED TO SIGNING RN IN BED 5 AT MIDTOWN.

Emergency response to residence under construction for a complaint of man down.  Upon arrival, pt (48 yoa M) found seated on steps of residence.  Pt stated that he had taken Suboxone earlier this date and now felt like ''ghosts were watching him''.  Pt stated he had a history of drug use.  Pt loaded and SAMPLE obtained.

During transport, baseline vitals obtained and found to be within limits.  ECG confirmed sinus rhythm.  Pt rode in a position of comfort for trip with no additional complaints.

Upon arrival, pt report and transfer of care.

EN28/B arrived on scene and found a male Pt. laying on the ground unresponsive. We checked his glucose which was 396 and assisted the Med unit in loading the Pt. Once the Pt. was loaded the Med unit released us from scene.

We were called to this location for the evaluation of a female pt that was in cardiac arrest. UOA we met with the patient as she was on the floor in an upstairs bedroom with CPR being performed by family member PTA. 
I placed patient on cardiac monitor as family member was continuing with chest compressions, initial rhythm noted was aysytolic. We secured patient to mega mover, carried her to awaiting cot, immediately continued chest compressions with ventilations not being performed until we secured her into unit.
As we secured her to LSB and cot with straps, OP airway was placed into airway and ventilations were began via BVM with the use of 10 lpm of O2. I then secured multi-function pads to chest, continued CPR and obtained IV access L AC 20 gauge cath x two sticks secured line and immediately began fluid bolus admin of NSS, 250 ml.
As IV was secured a total of Epi 1:10,000, 1 mg x 2 and one 2 mg Narcan admin throughout event during Tx. After admin of medications, King airway size 5 was inserted and secured into patients airway with use of waveform capnography also. 
The Tx to the ED was emergent, throughout the remainder of the Tx occasional pauses in CPR were performed to maintain proper protocol following for the asystolic rhythm treating with no changes in the rhythm being noted nor changes in the patients status. UOA at Skyline ED, we exited unit with patient, rolled her into ED, placed her on bed #2, gave full verbal report was given to staff releasing care. B. Petty

Responded emergency traffic to the charted address for a possible overdose. UAF a 43yo M laying supine in bed at his residence with a friend on scene, pt's friend stated the pt's daughter call 911 because her father sent text messages stated he was going to overdose on pills and insulin. Pt was responsive but would just state ''leave me alone''. Pt then stated he took ''a lot'' of insulin and the refused to answer anymore questions. Pt's airway is patent, breathing is non labored with good chest rise and fall, palpated strong and regular radials pulses, skin is intact PWD. When trying to load the pt onto the stretcher pt became combative, kicking and punching, pt was placed on the stretcher and secured with rails x 2 straps x 5. While en route pt stated he took some muscle relaxers but unknown how many. Pt would not follow commands a repeatedly removed his seat belts and cardiac monitor. Radio report was given to Summit ED without orders in return. Upon arrival to the ED pt was placed in room 3, pt was placed on the stretcher via draw sheet and secured with rails x 2. Bedside report was given to ED RN and MD, there was no change in pt condition upon turnover.
